channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: OpenAI’s Codex and Anthropic’s Claude spark coding revolution as developers say they’ve abandoned traditional programming
item.description: New releases from OpenAI and Anthropic sparked an existential crisis among coders, but many engineers say they stopped coding months ago.

item.title: This classic MTV website goes where Netflix dare not venture
item.description: Call it the day the music died. On December 31, 2025, MTV’s last music-only stations shut down forever​ . The last video played on MTV Music in the U....

item.title: 3 research-backed improv tricks to help manage stress
item.description: Stress isn’t just an occasional visitor in our lives—it’s more the houseguest who never got the hint to leave. Between economic uncertainty, workplace...

item.title: Why Spotify’s developers haven’t written new code in more than a month
item.description: Spotify’s most senior engineers don’t type code anymore. In fact, they have not written a single line of code since December, co-CEO Gustav Söderström...

item.title: Clandestine Romances Run Rampant in Today’s Workplace
item.description: It’s happening more than you think. These policies can help your company handle relationships between co-workers.

item.title: I Went to an AI Dating Cafe. Things Got Weird Fast
item.description: Going on dates with an AI is every bit as bizarre as you might expect. But boy do I feel good about my hair.

item.title: The ‘zombie internet’ has arrived—and it has devastating consequences for advertising, social media, and the human web
item.description: In late January, like Dr. Frankenstein pulling the knife switch to jolt his monster alive, entrepreneur Matt Schlicht flipped the digital switch on hi...

item.title: How leaders are protecting culture while AI rewrites how work gets done
item.description: At Ingka Group, the largest IKEA retailer in 32 countries, leaders set out to adopt AI in a way that wouldn’t put their culture at risk.

item.title: How Mass Layoffs Transformed LinkedIn Into the Internet’s New Thought Leadership Hub
item.description: For success on the platform, don’t just demonstrate your career progression—share your authentic expertise.

item.title: Apple Pushes Emergency iPhone Update After ‘Extremely Sophisticated’ Spyware Attack. Experts Say to Install Immediately
item.description: Apple’s latest update comes with an urgent ‘update now’ message.

item.title: The NHL Can’t Show Its Players on Twitter. So, They Found a Brilliant Solution
item.description: It’s a perfect example of legal constraints forcing creativity.

item.title: 41 popular hair extensions have toxic chemicals linked to cancer and other health risks
item.description: The global market for hair extensions is booming and is projected to hit $14 billion in annual sales by 2028 . What was once a niche luxury item for w...

item.title: Bank of America lifts Moynihan’s pay 17% to $41 million for 2025
item.description: A year ago, Moynihan’s compensation was increased 21% to $35 million after the bank was able to boost its earnings.

item.title: Marc Andreessen made a dire software prediction 15 years ago. Now it’s happening in a way nobody imagined
item.description: The billionaire venture capitalist explained why software would eat the world in 2011. He had no idea.

item.title: Trump says regime change in Iran that ousts Islamic clerics ‘would be the best thing that could happen’ as another carrier heads to Mideast
item.description: Trump said the USS Gerald R. Ford, the world's largest aircraft carrier, is being sent from the Caribbean Sea to the Mideast.
